# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account and generates a JSON key file for application authentication. Include the key creation in the template so applications can authenticate immediately. This is for our application services that need GCP API access from outside GCP.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# Generated by scripts/generate_deploymentmanager_template.py (modified) at Wed Oct 13 08:25:46 UTC 2022
parameters:
- name: projectId
  type: string
  description: The GCP Project ID where resources will be created in this template are placed on the specified ProjectID. Must match exactly one of your existing Google Cloud Platform projects (https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/projects). When deploying, it must also exist as a project with sufficient ownership privileges and permissions to create new services or objects like Compute Engine instances in service account' `access_key` .
  default: mygcpapporg # Required Parameter when creating GCP Service Account. Replace 'mygcpapp org' below for unique name requirement per your organization, then provide the ProjectId e.g., projects/PROJECT-ID where PROJECT ID is provided above and set it in script as parameter
resources: []  // List of Resources that will be deployed under this template. The Manager currently does not deploy any resources at present to save space here for larger templates but you can add them with the appropriate parameters, like computeEngineInstances or networkEndpoints etc.. after defining all necessary custom resource Definitions in your project
